enonce.texte:
Read the following text about cultural diversity and answer the questions below.

Cultural Diversity in Cameroon

Cameroon is a country with rich cultural diversity. There are about 250 ethnic groups, each with its own language, traditions, and customs. The Bamileke people are known for their beautiful handicrafts and colourful dances. In the North, you will find the Fulani people who are known for their cattle herding and Islamic traditions. The Bassa people, who live in the coastal regions, are famous for their music and fishing skills. Cultural diversity is important because it helps people understand each other better. When we learn about other cultures, we become more tolerant and respectful. Many festivals are organized to celebrate this diversity. During these festivals, people wear their traditional clothes, dance, and share their traditional food. This brings the community together and strengthens national unity.

questions.texte: How many ethnic groups are there in Cameroon according to the text?
questions.reponse: According to the text, there are about 250 ethnic groups in Cameroon.

questions.texte: What are the Fulani people known for?
questions.reponse: The Fulani people are known for their cattle herding and Islamic traditions.

questions.texte: Why is cultural diversity important according to the text?
questions.reponse: Cultural diversity is important because it helps people understand each other better, become more tolerant and respectful, and strengthens national unity.

questions.texte: What do people do during cultural festivals according to the text?
questions.reponse: During cultural festivals, people wear their traditional clothes, dance, and share their traditional food.

intitule: Asking for Information about Cultural Practices

enonce.texte: Complete the following questions about cultural practices using the correct question words: what, where, when, who, why, how.

questions.texte: ______ do people celebrate the traditional festival in your village?
______ is the traditional dance performed at weddings?
______ do people wear traditional clothes during celebrations?
______ cooks the traditional food during the ceremony?
______ do people celebrate the new harvest?
______ is the traditional food prepared in your culture?

intitule: Writing a Paragraph about a Famous Leader

enonce.texte:
Write a short paragraph (80-100 words) about a famous African or world leader. In your paragraph, include:

  • The name of the leader
  • Their country of origin
  • Why they are famous
  • What you admire about them
  • At least two adjectives to describe the leader

questions.texte: Write the paragraph about the famous leader.
questions.reponse:
Nelson Mandela

Nelson Mandela was a remarkable leader from South Africa. He is famous for fighting against apartheid and promoting equality for all people. He spent 27 years in prison for his beliefs, but he never gave up. He became the first black president of South Africa in 1994. I admire his courage and determination to fight for justice. He was a wise and peaceful leader who believed in forgiveness and reconciliation. His life story teaches us that we can overcome challenges if we are persistent and believe in what is right. He is a true inspiration to people all over the world.

intitule: Giving a Presentation on Cultural Diversity

enonce.texte:
You have been asked to give a short presentation to your classmates about cultural diversity in your community. Write the text of your presentation (80-100 words). Include:

  • Examples of cultural practices in your community
  • Why cultural diversity is important
  • How people can learn about other cultures

questions.texte: Write the presentation text about cultural diversity.
questions.reponse:
Cultural Diversity in My Community

Good morning everyone. Today, I want to talk about cultural diversity in my community. We have many ethnic groups with different traditions. For example, we celebrate weddings, funerals, and harvest festivals in different ways. We also have different types of traditional food, dances, and clothing. Cultural diversity is important because it teaches us to respect each other. When we understand other cultures, we become more tolerant and peaceful. To learn about other cultures, we can attend festivals, read books, or talk to people from different backgrounds. I believe that cultural diversity makes our community stronger and more interesting. Thank you for listening.

enonce.texte:
Complete the following dialogue between two students about cultural diversity. Use appropriate questions and answers to exchange information about cultural practices.

Marie: Hi Paul! ______?

Paul: I come from a village in the North. We have many interesting customs.

Marie: ______?

Paul: We celebrate the harvest festival every year. It is a very important event.

Marie: ______?

Paul: People wear traditional clothes, dance, and eat special food during the festival.

Marie: That sounds wonderful! ______?

Paul: Yes, everyone in the community participates, including children and the elderly.

Marie: ______?

Paul: I think cultural diversity is important because it makes our country unique and interesting.

questions.texte: Complete the dialogue with appropriate questions and answers.
questions.reponse:
Marie: Hi Paul! Where do you come from?

Paul: I come from a village in the North. We have many interesting customs.

Marie: What do you celebrate in your community?

Paul: We celebrate the harvest festival every year. It is a very important event.

Marie: How do people celebrate this festival?

Paul: People wear traditional clothes, dance, and eat special food during the festival.

Marie: That sounds wonderful! Does everyone in the community participate?

Paul: Yes, everyone in the community participates, including children and the elderly.

Marie: Why do you think cultural diversity is important?

Paul: I think cultural diversity is important because it makes our country unique and interesting.

enonce.texte: Write a short text comparing two different cultural practices that you know about. For example, compare a traditional wedding ceremony in one culture with another, or compare how different cultures celebrate festivals. Your text should be about 80-100 words.

questions.texte: Write the comparison of two cultural practices.
questions.reponse:
Comparing Two Cultural Practices

In the Bamilike culture, weddings are very colourful events. The groom's family visits the bride's family to present gifts. The bride wears a special dress called 'Toghu' which is very beautiful. In the Fulani culture, weddings are more simple. The ceremony often takes place in a mosque, and the bride wears a white dress. Both cultures celebrate weddings with music and dancing. However, the Bamilike wedding often lasts longer and involves more traditional rituals. Both cultures value the union of two families and celebrate with joy. These differences make each culture unique and special.
